events.title: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
events.description: Xi Jinping urges authorities to ‘spare no effort’ in rescue operation after explosion in northern Shanxi province At least 82 people have been killed in a gas explosion at a coalmine in northern China’s Shanxi province, in the country’s worst mining disaster in 17 years. The explosion happened on Friday at 7.29pm (12.29 BST) while 247 workers were underground at the Liushenyu coalmine in Qinyuan county, the state media agency Xinhua reported. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast

events.title: WHO puts suspected Ebola deaths at 220, warns outbreak is outpacing response efforts
events.description: The World Health Organization warned Monday that a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo and neighbouring Uganda was outpacing response efforts, with at least 220 suspected deaths reported. Health workers in the conflict-hit Ituri province face attacks on medical facilities, complicating efforts to contain the rare Bundibugyo strain of the virus.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO puts suspected Ebola deaths at 220, warns outbreak is outpacing response efforts

events.title: Nuclear issue remains key obstacle in Iran-US deal
events.description: Simon Mabon, Professor of International Politics and Middle East Studies at Lancaster University, joins FRANCE 24 to discuss the latest developments in the Middle East. While Iran and the United States are still exploring a possible deal to end the conflict, both sides are now downplaying hopes of an imminent breakthrough after appearing close to an agreement just days ago. Mabon explains that Iran may currently hold leverage through its influence over the Strait of Hormuz, increasing pressure on both President Donald Trump and the global economy, while Tehran also seeks economic guarantees after years of sanctions. The Iranian nuclear programme remains a major obstacle, with Israel likely pushing behind the scenes against any agreement that fails to address it directly.

events.title: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
events.description: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuations Heavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of Bengal, South China Sea and Pacific Ocean. Given the accompanying low wind speeds, high daily and hourly rainfall totals have been recorded, with as much as 75mm locally in Hunan, 85mm in Anhui in a 24-hour period, and 95mm on the island of Hainan. Twelve people have died because of the flooding and hundreds of residents have been evacuated by emergency services. There were major travel disruptions, electricity outages, as well as school and business closures. Social media posts showed submerged cars and people fishing along flooded streets. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China

events.title: Russia deploys hypersonic missile in latest major strikes on Ukraine
events.description: Kyiv is still dealing with the aftermath of a large-scale Russian overnight strike that killed four people and injured dozens, with emergency teams continuing search and cleanup operations across the city. More than 50 sites were hit, including homes, a school and a museum, while Russia says it used its Oreshnik hypersonic missile for only the third time in the war. Moscow describes the attacks as retaliation, while Ukraine denies targeting civilians and says its strikes focus on military infrastructure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia deploys hypersonic missile in latest major strikes on Ukraine

events.title: WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness
events.description: The recent Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks demonstrate that the world is still vulnerable to rapidly spreading infectious diseases, Tedros Ghebreyesus, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), warned on Saturday at the close of the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness

events.title: Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast
events.description: The 39-year-old was fatally injured at a shoal off the Cassowary Coast in Queensland, apparently while spearfishing.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast

events.title: One killed and many feared trapped under collapsed building in Philippines
events.description: A Malaysian man has died and about 20 people are feared trapped at the site near Manila, officials say.
events.ticker_label: 💥 One killed and many feared trapped under collapsed building in Philippines

events.title: Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court
events.description: The UN International Court of Justice (ICJ) on Thursday ruled that the right to strike is protected under a core International Labour Organization (ILO) convention, in a landmark advisory opinion settling a long-running dispute between workers and employers worldwide.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court

events.title: Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows
events.description: More than 20,000 attacks on markets, farmland and food distribution systems have been recorded since 2018 Hunger is being increasingly exploited as a weapon of war with more than 20,000 documented incidents of “food-related violence” in the past eight years, new analysis reveals. Attacks include 1,261 strikes on markets used by families for daily groceries and 863 incidents in which food distribution systems were targeted and workers killed. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows

events.title: Suspected Ebola cases in DRC pass 900 as health workers face attacks and shortages
events.description: WHO says outbreak poses ‘very high’ risk for country, but risk of disease spreading globally remains low Authorities in the Democratic Republic of the Congo say that suspected Ebola cases have now passed 900 in the ongoing outbreak in the east of the country. The DRC’s ministry of communication said in a post on X on Sunday that there were 904 suspected cases and 119 suspected deaths. Continue reading...

events.title: Baby pulled from vehicle trapped in raging floodwaters in dramatic rescue caught on video
events.description: A dramatic rescue unfolded in Beeville, Texas, as an officer pulled a baby from a vehicle being swept toward a creek by rising floodwaters Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Baby pulled from vehicle trapped in raging floodwaters in dramatic rescue caught on video

events.title: Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse
events.description: Inmates at Barinas prison allege they were peacefully protesting when prison staff opened fire, leaving some wounded Inmates at Venezuela’s western Barinas prison staged a protest on its roof on Sunday, piling flaming mattresses and calling for the removal of the facility’s director, whom they accused of overseeing guards as they shot unarmed prisoners. “We want justice. They are shooting us, the guards and the wardens,” a prisoner said in a video shared by the Venezuelan Observatory of Prisons, a local NGO, on X, in which a man is seen with a bullet wound in his chest. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse

events.title: WHO head 'deeply concerned' over 'scale and speed' of Ebola spread, says emergency committee will meet
events.description: WHO chief convenes emergency committee over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda, citing over 500 suspected cases and 131 fatalities from the virus.

events.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
events.description: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate action The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediate action. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO

events.title: Thai rescuers join effort to free seven people trapped in Laos cave
events.description: Group have been stuck in flooded cave in central Laos for five days after heavy rain caused landslides Divers who helped in the dramatic rescue of a young Thai football team in 2018 have joined efforts to free seven people who have been trapped for five days inside a remote, flooded cave in central Laos. The group entered the cave in Xaysomboun province on Wednesday to hunt for wildlife and search for gold, reports suggest. Heavy rain led to landslides, which blocked the cave entrance. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Thai rescuers join effort to free seven people trapped in Laos cave

events.title: At least 82 killed after massive gas explosion rips through coal mine in China
events.description: A massive gas explosion ripped through a coal mine in China's Shanxi province, killing at least 82 people and hospitalizing 120 others, the AP reports.

events.title: 4 dead, 17 still missing in collapse of unfinished hotel in Philippines
events.description: Rescuers have pulled three people from the rubble of a collapsed nine-story hotel under construction in Angeles City, Philippines

events.title: Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk
events.description: The United Nations voiced alarm on Friday over reports of an overnight attack on a vocational school and dormitory in the town of Starobilsk in Ukraine’s Luhansk region which killed and injured multiple civilians, including children.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk

events.title: General Assembly backs historic World Court climate crisis ruling
events.description: A landmark General Assembly resolution adopted on Wednesday is “a powerful affirmation” of international law, climate justice and science, according to UN chief António Guterres.

events.title: Australian spy boss says he shifted resources from counterterrorism before attack
events.description: An Australian spy agency boss has told an inquiry he had pivoted resources away from counterterrorism to espionage and foreign a few years before two gunmen massacred 15 people at Sydney Hanukkah celebration
events.ticker_label: 💥 Australian spy boss says he shifted resources from counterterrorism before attack

events.title: Peacekeeping missions at risk due to cuts, tensions — report
events.description: A "perfect storm" of falling troop numbers, geopolitical tensions and funding crises is jeopardizing international peacekeeping efforts, a new study has warned.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Peacekeeping missions at risk due to cuts, tensions — report

events.title: Pope Leo warns AI must be “disarmed” in landmark encyclical
events.description: Pope Leo XIV personally presented his first encyclical at the Vatican, outlining a strong warning about artificial intelligence and its impact on humanity. He argued that AI should be “disarmed,” warning it could deepen global conflicts and dehumanise society, while calling for broader regulation and diverse voices in shaping its development. Joined by Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah, the Pope framed AI as a defining challenge of the modern era, comparable to the Industrial Revolution.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo warns AI must be “disarmed” in landmark encyclical

events.title: Ukraine: Kyiv residents endure 'worst night of the year' over the weekend
events.description: Rescue teams are still working to stabilise buildings in Kyiv after massive Russian strikes over the weekend. Residents described it as the worst night of the year so far, with a high number of casualties. According to our correspondent Gulliver Cragg, the attacks appeared to target the city centre but were largely repelled thanks to air defence systems.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Kyiv residents endure 'worst night of the year' over the weekend

events.title: Possible Iran-US deal: What we know about the key issues on both sides
events.description: The United States and Iran have downplayed prospects for a rapid deal to end the Middle East war, despite signs of progress in talks. Key sticking points remain over Iran’s nuclear programme, control of the Strait of Hormuz, sanctions relief and a possible ceasefire in Lebanon, with both sides signalling that major issues are still unresolved.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Possible Iran-US deal: What we know about the key issues on both sides

events.title: Russia threatens more Kyiv strikes and tells foreign nationals to leave
events.description: It comes after the Ukrainian capital suffered one of the biggest aerial assaults of the war overnight on Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia threatens more Kyiv strikes and tells foreign nationals to leave

events.title: Laos: Rescuers race to reach 7 trapped in flooded cave
events.description: The villagers were searching for gold in the cave when flash flooding blocked their way out. The rescue effort includes Thai experts who aided in the dramatic recovery of a youth soccer team from a flooded cave in 2018.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Laos: Rescuers race to reach 7 trapped in flooded cave

events.title: The BHP files: World’s biggest miner BHP backtracks on climate action with key projects put on ice, leaked documents reveal
events.description: Exclusive: Cache of internal documents leaked to the Guardian and the ABC’s Four Corners show multinational has war-gamed ways to massively delay decarbonisation Revealed: the internal BHP memo that slammed the brakes on world’s biggest miner’s climate push Read more from the BHP files investigation here Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The world’s biggest miner has halted or delayed projects to cut vast amounts of emissions and has quietly war-gamed options to push major climate investments in its Western Australian iron ore operations into the next two decades, internal documents show. An exclusive investigation based on documents leaked to the Guardian and the ABC’s Four Corners can reveal that BHP , one of Australia’s biggest historic emitters, has dumped plans for a facility that could have significantly reduced emissions and has put on ice renewable projects designed to power its iron ore operations in the vast, resource-rich Pilbara region. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The BHP files: World’s biggest miner BHP backtracks on climate action with key projects put on ice, leaked documents reveal

events.title: Haitian children ‘paying the highest price’ amid surge in gang recruitment
events.description: As gangs continue to “terrorise” communities in Haiti, children are the ones paying the highest price, the UN Special Representative for Children and Armed Conflict warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Haitian children ‘paying the highest price’ amid surge in gang recruitment

events.title: 28 Boy Scouts rescued from fast-moving floodwaters during river trip in West Virginia
events.description: A routine Boy Scout river trip turned into a rescue mission when floodwaters trapped 28 scouts along the Cacapon River in West Virginia Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 28 Boy Scouts rescued from fast-moving floodwaters during river trip in West Virginia

events.title: US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv issued a security alert warning of a potentially significant air attack that may occur within the next 24 hours in Ukraine.

events.title: Three killed in Uganda after crashing into elephant
events.description: Four people were also injured in the incident inside Murchison Falls National Park, officials say.

events.title: Russia warns foreigners to leave Kyiv as it prepares ‘systematic strikes’
events.description: Moscow calls upcoming 'systematic strikes' on Ukrainian capital a response to Kyiv's recent attack in Luhansk.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia warns foreigners to leave Kyiv as it prepares ‘systematic strikes’

events.title: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
events.description: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’

events.title: World Urban Forum backs ‘Baku Call to Action’ on global housing crisis
events.description: A landmark gathering of more than 57,000 participants – the largest in the history of the World Urban Forum – closed on Friday in Baku with an urgent call to rethink how the world houses its people, as a new roadmap urges governments, cities and communities to act collectively on a crisis affecting billions.

events.title: BHP defies its own climate strategy to spend hundreds of millions on polluting diesel trucks in Pilbara
events.description: Exclusive: Mining giant says technology is not yet advanced enough to run a fully electrified fleet but experts say it is hooked on federal fuel tax credits Revealed: the internal BHP memo that slammed the brakes on world’s biggest miner’s climate push Read more from the BHP files investigation here Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast BHP has continued to spend hundreds of millions of dollars buying diesel trucks in the Pilbara despite internal documents suggesting it would increase emissions and be “misaligned” with its decarbonisation goals. The mining giant is Australia’s biggest consumer of diesel and trucks are its biggest single source of diesel emissions. Replacing the fleet with battery-electric trucks is considered a critical step in the multinational’s efforts to decarbonise. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 BHP defies its own climate strategy to spend hundreds of millions on polluting diesel trucks in Pilbara

events.title: At least four dead and 17 missing after unfinished building collapses in the Philippines
events.description: At least four people have died after an unfinished building collapsed in the Philippines, authorities in the country have said.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official
events.description: The Security Council is meeting at the request of Russia which on Friday accused Ukraine of targeting a student dormitory overnight in the occupied Luhansk region, reportedly killing six people – including children – and injuring dozens. Kyiv has denied targeting the civilian building, saying it had struck a Russian military drone command headquarters, according to news reports. Follow live coverage below and for full meetings coverage of the council, click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official

events.title: China mine blast: Search and investigation continue
events.description: Chinese authorities have launched an investigation into "serious breaches" of security regulations after an explosion in a coal mine left at least 82 people dead. Two people remain missing.

events.title: Ebola risk ‘very high’ in eastern DR Congo as UN intensifies response
events.description: The UN is rushing emergency personnel, funding and supplies into eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to counter the fast-growing Ebola outbreak spreading through conflict-ravaged provinces.

events.title: Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23
events.description: At least 23 killed and 70 wounded after a suicide bomber reportedly detonated an explosives-laden vehicle alongside an incoming passenger train in Quetta, Pakistan.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23

events.title: 'My body carried me,' Elizabeth Smart says. Now she's celebrating it
events.description: Her abduction at age 14 drew international attention. After her rescue, Smart says she struggled with feeling shame around her body. Bodybuilding has helped her see herself differently.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'My body carried me,' Elizabeth Smart says. Now she's celebrating it

events.title: Parts of Europe swelter in record May heat as deaths at sports events spur warnings

events.title: Newsom declares state of emergency in Orange County as failing chemical tank nears catastrophic explosion
events.description: Emergency crews launch overnight mission to neutralize a volatile chemical tank at a Southern California aerospace facility in Garden Grove as Gov. Gavin Newsom declares state of emergency.

events.title: Germany news: More than 1,900 missing children
events.description: Authorities say there are 1,933 missing children cases nationwide, up 6.8% from the year before. And, travellers in Germany seem nervous when it comes to air travel, amid the war in Iran, a survey suggests. Follow DW.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany news: More than 1,900 missing children

events.title: Gun suspect killed after opening fire at White House security checkpoint
events.description: The suspect in a shooting near the White House has died after being shot by police officers, according to the US Secret Service (USSS).
events.ticker_label: 💥 Gun suspect killed after opening fire at White House security checkpoint

events.title: War is becoming a death sentence for women and girls
events.description: Thirteen-year-old Mona still remembers the moment the second airstrike hit.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War is becoming a death sentence for women and girls

events.title: Building the smart city: Promise, pitfalls and the people at its heart
events.description: From AI-powered transit systems to digital twins and flood-proof parks, cities are embracing technology at an unprecedented scale. But as innovation accelerates, experts warn that inclusion, trust and security will determine who truly benefits.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Building the smart city: Promise, pitfalls and the people at its heart

events.title: Teen sailor killed aboard USS West Virginia at Pearl Harbor identified after 82 years through DNA analysis
events.description: The remains of Fireman Third Class Royle Luker, killed during the 1941 Pearl Harbor attack, are returning to Arkansas for burial with military honors.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Teen sailor killed aboard USS West Virginia at Pearl Harbor identified after 82 years through DNA analysis

events.title: WHO chief says suspected Ebola deaths at 220 as epidemic ‘outpacing us’
events.description: Tedros ⁠Adhanom Ghebreyesus says ​a delay in detecting cases means responders were now 'playing catch-up'.

events.title: Could Israel sabotage US-Iran deal?
events.description: As the US and Iran move closer to a peace deal, Israel vows to keep striking what it calls regional and nuclear threats.

events.title: Millions gather in Mecca for Hajj despite security concerns
events.description: Despite security concerns linked to the conflict in the Middle East, pilgrims from around the world continue to arrive in Mecca for this year’s Hajj. Saudi officials say more than 1.5 million people have already reached the kingdom, with numbers expected to rise further as many say they are willing to take the risk to complete the pilgrimage.

events.title: Middle East: Deep mistrust clouds US-Iran negotiations
events.description: Rouzbeh Parsi, Adjunct Senior Lecturer at Lund University, is FRANCE 24’s guest to discuss the evolving situation in the Middle East. Iran and the United States are both seeking a possible diplomatic way out of the conflict, but uncertainty remains high amid conflicting signals from both sides. Parsi highlights the contradictions in the current negotiations and the difficulty in finding a credible agreement, particularly as mistrust continues to dominate relations. While the risk of escalation into a wider war remains, both Washington and Tehran appear to be looking for an exit strategy from prolonged confrontation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East: Deep mistrust clouds US-Iran negotiations

events.title: Authorities investigate safety lapses after China coal mine blast kills at least 82
events.description: Authorities in northern China are investigating a coal mine operator for safety lapses after an explosion killed at least 82 people

events.title: The world urgently needs a US-Iran deal now
events.description: Without a deal between Washington and Tehran, the fallout from Strait of Hormuz closure could deepen global crises.

events.title: Muslims begin the annual Hajj against a backdrop of war concerns
events.description: More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Muslims begin the annual Hajj against a backdrop of war concerns

events.title: Uganda confirms 2 new Ebola cases, DRC infections hit 900
events.description: The two new cases are health workers in the Ugandan capital, Kampala. The WHO has declared a public health emergency over the latest outbreak, which began in neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo.

events.title: DR Congo: Authorities struggle to contain fast-moving Ebola outbreak
events.description: Uganda has confirmed two new Ebola cases among healthcare workers in Kampala, bringing the country’s total to seven, all linked to the outbreak in neighbouring Democratic Republic of Congo. In eastern Congo, where more than 900 suspected cases have been identified, authorities and volunteers are struggling to contain the spread amid limited resources, ongoing violence, and the absence of a vaccine or treatment for the rare Bundibugyo strain.
events.ticker_label: 💥 DR Congo: Authorities struggle to contain fast-moving Ebola outbreak

events.title: Settler violence in the West Bank intensifies against Palestinians
events.description: In the occupied West Bank, illegal Israeli settlements are dramatically increasing, encouraged by the Israeli government and protected by the army. Meanwhile, Palestinians are suffering greater numbers of violent attacks by extremist settlers, for which they almost always receive no justice. Our France 2 colleagues report, with FRANCE 24's Florent Marchais.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Settler violence in the West Bank intensifies against Palestinians

events.title: France swelters as first-ever May heatwave alert issued
events.description: France experienced record high temperatures for May over the weekend, according to the country’s national weather service, with Paris recording this year's first temperature above 30C on Saturday. A spokesperson for Meteo France added that a yellow heatwave alert issued Sunday was the very first “to be issued in May since the system was created in 2004".

events.title: World News in Brief: Lives lost and aid destroyed in Ukraine, rainfall alert for Horn of Africa, $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees
events.description: The Representative of the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, in Ukraine has strongly condemned the deadly and destructive Russian missile and drone strikes in Dnipro on Tuesday night.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Lives lost and aid destroyed in Ukraine, rainfall alert for Horn of Africa, $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees

events.title: Two suspected American communist insurgents killed in clash in the Philippines
events.description: Lyle Prijoles and Kai Dana-Rene Sorem were among 19 killed in a Philippine army firefight with suspected communist insurgents tied to the New People’s Army.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two suspected American communist insurgents killed in clash in the Philippines

events.title: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence
events.description: Alberta premier calls for referendum on secession after judge ruled initiative to force binding vote invalid The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney , has said that Alberta is “essential” to the country’s future, hours after the province’s leader moved the oil-rich region closer toward a referendum on independence. Separatists in the western province spent months collecting signatures seeking to trigger a binding October vote on seceding from the nation. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence

events.title: Las Vegas Enhanced Games launch sparks controversy and ethical debate
events.description: Athletes using performance-enhancing drugs under medical supervision in an attempt to break records : that is the concept behind the Enhanced Games currently taking place in Las Vegas. But the open use of banned substances is already fuelling major controversy, with many experts warning that the long-term effects of these drugs on the body remain largely unknown. The Games are also attracting huge amounts of money through sponsorships and sports betting, as our correspondent reports from Las Vegas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Las Vegas Enhanced Games launch sparks controversy and ethical debate

events.title: Khalida Popal and Javier Zanetti among football stars appointed UN Champions
events.description: With the World Cup just days away, 16 greats of the game have been appointed Football for the Goals (FFTG) Champions, using their profiles to raise awareness of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and advocate for a better future for people and the planet.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Khalida Popal and Javier Zanetti among football stars appointed UN Champions

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Ambassadors debate future of Gaza amid stalled progress on lasting peace
events.description: The Security Council is discussing the stalled progress in Gaza as the fragile ceasefire there limps on, and deteriorating conditions across the West Bank amid continued civilian casualties and mounting humanitarian needs. Discussions are likely to focus on who governs Gaza, and how - with no disarmament in sight, together with efforts to support recovery.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Ambassadors debate future of Gaza amid stalled progress on lasting peace

events.title: Trump says a deal to end war with Iran is near, but many questions remain
events.description: U.S. and Iran are nearing a tentative deal to end the conflict, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and curb Tehran's uranium stockpile — though major details remain unresolved.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump says a deal to end war with Iran is near, but many questions remain

events.title: Afghanistan's crisis deepens hidden violence against women
events.description: As poverty deepens and the Taliban curtails women's rights, domestic violence in Afghanistan is becoming more dangerous, less visible and harder to escape.

events.title: Cuba receives China rice shipment amid US threats, blackouts
events.description: Chinese aid is expected to alleviate food shortages in Cuba. But the island's economic crisis continues with blackouts and oil embargos are compunded by threats from the United States.

events.title: Hypersonic missile used in Kyiv strike: What we know about it

events.title: Spearfisher mauled in Australia's second fatal shark attack in a week
events.description: A spearfisher has been mauled on the Great Barrier Reef in Australia’s second fatal shark attack in just over a week

events.title: Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground
events.description: Dire conditions in Gaza marked by continuing violence, rodent infestations and the spread of infectious disease are being made worse by blockages of essential medical supplies, UN agencies warned on Friday.

events.title: Gaza risks ‘permanent’ state of limbo if transition plan stalls, Security Council hears
events.description: As Gaza’s fragile ceasefire frays and humanitarian conditions deteriorate, a senior UN envoy warned the Security Council on Thursday that delays in implementing the Council-backed transition plan for the enclave will only increase suffering and undermine recovery.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García
events.description: Judge said Trump administration would not have prosecuted Ábrego had he not challenged his high-profile deportation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive for mass deportations when he was sent to the notorious anti-terrorism mega-prison in El Salvador known as Cecot in March 2025. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García

events.title: At four, her head was shaved and her clothes burned. Aunty Lorraine doesn’t want her trauma to be forgotten
events.description: The now 88-year-old is urging Australian governments to throw their support behind a new national plan for Stolen Generations survivors as they enter their final years Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Aunty Lorraine Peeters only remembers the metal gates opening as she was driven away from her home, at Brewarrina mission in north-west New South Wales. She was taken, along with her brothers and sisters, at just four years old. Her home for the next six years would be the Cootamundra Aboriginal Girls Home, where she was separated from her siblings, trained as a domestic servant and systematically brainwashed to be white. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At four, her head was shaved and her clothes burned. Aunty Lorraine doesn’t want her trauma to be forgotten

events.title: BHP quietly scrapped plan to build Pilbara plant that would have drastically cut emissions
events.description: Exclusive: Jimblebar processing facility would have produced higher quality iron ore sought by steelmakers around the world – themselves under pressure to curb pollution World’s biggest miner BHP backtracks on climate action with key projects put on ice, leaked documents reveal Read more from the BHP files investigation here Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast BHP quietly dumped plans for an iron ore processing facility that would have cut emissions drastically, despite internally rating it as having “excellent social value” and being “well-aligned” to its shareholder-endorsed climate plan and decarbonisation targets. In 2025 the mining giant was well advanced in its plans to build a beneficiation plant near its Jimblebar open-cut mine in the Pilbara, which would greatly improve the purity and quality of its iron ore. Continue reading...

events.title: US arms sales to Taiwan on ‘pause’ due to Iran war, says acting navy chief

When asked at a congressional hearing on Thursday about a $14bn (£10.4bn) weapons package awaiting Trump’s signoff for months, Hung Cao said: “Right now we’re doing a pause in order to make sure we have the munitions we need for Epic Fury [the Iran war] – which we have plenty.” Continue reading...

The Gaza Sunbirds are a team of people, often athletes, who are dedicated to bringing food to those who need it most. They deliver food across the war-torn enclave by bicycle, working alongside other charities. We spoke to one of the co-founders, Karim Ali, in Perspective.

events.title: ‘She does not back down’: the couple seeking to legalise same-sex marriage in Botswana
events.description: Bonolo Selelo and Tsholofelo Kumile are going to court for right to wed but face fierce opposition from church groups Bonolo Selelo was at Botswana’s national museum for a Gaborone Pride event when she spotted Tsholofelo Kumile and was struck by her good looks. The two initiated a conversation and when Kumile expressed anxiety about what a tarot reading at the event might hold, Selelo thought nothing of offering her a hug. The reading turned out positive but Kumile claimed her hug anyway and they talked for hours. That was 1 October 2023. Two months later, they moved in together. Then, on a hike during the Easter holidays in 2024, Selelo proposed to Kumile. A year later, they visited a local government office to register their intent to marry and were told it wasn’t legal. Continue reading...

events.title: Venezuelan makeup artist who was deported to El Salvador seeks asylum in Spain: ‘I feel safe here’
events.description: Andry José Hernández Romero was deported from the US to El Salvador’s notorious Cecot prison before he was allowed to return to Venezuela One of the Venezuelan men sent from the US to El Salvador’s most notorious prison by Donald Trump has moved to Spain to request asylum after concluding that he did not feel safe back home and did not trust US authorities sufficiently to return to fight his legal case. Andry José Hernández Romero left Venezuela for Spain in early February and is due for his first asylum hearing in court there in a few days, hoping that the country’s liberal approach to immigration will afford him kinder treatment than the US or his own country had provided him, he revealed to the Guardian in his first interview since leaving for Europe. events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: Venezuela's government faces calls for elections after failed release of prisoners
events.description: After Venezuela's promised release of hundreds of political prisoners failed to materialize, opponents of the current U.S.-backed interim government demand their freedom and call for elections.

events.title: China launches Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 1 of 3 astronauts set for yearlong stay
events.description: China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.

events.title: Remains of lost arctic explorers identified with DNA, nearly two centuries later
events.description: New research shines a light on the lost Franklin Expedition, a 19th century voyage to the Canadian Arctic gone awry.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Remains of lost arctic explorers identified with DNA, nearly two centuries later

events.title: Chinese human rights cases in limbo after Trump-Xi summit
events.description: Trump and Xi said they had discussed political prisoners in China, including Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai. Ahead of Xi's September visit to Washington, advocates are urging the US to apply more pressure on China.

events.title: Germany's Dr. Wolff cosmetics company is going all in on AI
events.description: This medium-sized, family-run business has embraced artificial intelligence to stay competitive. All employees are encouraged to learn about and apply LLMs.

events.title: Turkish police evict ousted CHP leaders from party HQ
events.description: Police forced their way into the opposition party's headquarters, ending a standoff between ousted CHP leader Ozgur Ozel and supporters of court-appointed Kemal Kilicdaroglu.

events.title: Tennis: Wawrinka says goodbye as Swiatek advances at Roland-Garros
events.description: At Roland-Garros, Stan Wawrinka bowed out at 41 in an emotional farewell to the French Open, marking the end of a long and celebrated career at the tournament. Iga Swiatek continued her dominant run, while Gaël Monfils is set to play later tonight in what could be his final appearance at the event ahead of retirement. FRANCE 24's James Vasina has the latest.

events.title: Pope Leo’s first encyclical tackles artificial intelligence
events.description: On Monday, Pope Leo XIV unveiled his broad vision for the relationship between artificial intelligence and humanity in his first encyclical, a major papal letter addressed to bishops of the Roman Catholic Church. The document was presented alongside Chris Olah from Anthropic, in what appeared to be an effort to encourage dialogue between AI researchers and wider society. FRANCE 24's correspondent in Rome, Hannah Roberts, has the details.

events.title: Wawrinka bids an emotional adieu to Roland Garros in first-round defeat
events.description: Former French Open champion Stan Wawrinka bade an emotional farewell to Roland Garros on Monday, falling in four sets to Dutch player Jesper de Jong in what was his final appearance at the Paris grand slam. The 41-year-old Swiss, who won the title in 2015, received a heartfelt tribute from fans and tennis greats after his first-round exit.

events.title: More than 20 people taken to hospital after unknown substance sprayed in Tokyo
events.description: More than 20 people have been taken to hospital after someone sprayed an unknown substance in Tokyo, authorities said.

events.title: China launches astronauts into space - as lunar race intensifies
events.description: China has launched the Shenzhou 23 rocket into space carrying three astronauts, in a mission that will take Beijing closer to its ambition for a moon landing in 2030.

events.title: RAF jet flying defence secretary has signal jammed near Russian border
events.description: An RAF jet which the defence secretary was travelling in had its signals jammed as it flew near the Russian border, in what a defence source said was "reckless" interference.

events.title: Guide Kenton Cool scales Everest for the 20th time and says not ready to quit yet
events.description: British mountain guide Kenton Cool has scaled Mount Everest for the 20th time, breaking his own record for the most ascents by a non-Sherpa guide

events.title: Pope calls for robust regulation of AI in manifesto
events.description: Pope Leo XIV has called for robust regulation of artificial intelligence and for its developers to work for the common good rather than profit

events.title: Truck overturns in central Bangladesh, killing at least 15 people
events.description: A truck carrying iron rods and extra passengers has overturned in central Bangladesh, killing at least 15 people and injuring 10 others

events.title: Pope Leo XIV makes historic apology for Holy See's own role in legitimizing slavery
events.description: Pope Leo XIV has made a historic apology for the role the Holy See itself played in legitimizing slavery

events.title: UFO insider claims US has bodies of 4 different alien species from downed spacecraft in government custody
events.description: Dr. Hal Puthoff claims the U.S. has recovered remains of four separate species of alien life from UFOs, citing people involved in the recoveries.

events.title: Officials say possible crack in unstable chemical tank may relieve pressure at aerospace plant
events.description: Crews at GKN Aerospace in Garden Grove discover a possible crack in the unstable chemical tank that officials say may be relieving pressure inside.

events.title: Ritzy private academy attended by Kardashian kids faces trial in bizarre 'kissing club' scandal
events.description: A judge ruled that a lawsuit accusing Sierra Canyon School of ignoring an alleged student-run "kissing club" involving sexual abuse will proceed publicly.

events.title: Key names to know as Murdaugh double-murder case gets stunning reset
events.description: Alex Murdaugh faces a murder retrial after a court found clerk Becky Hill improperly influenced jurors. Here are the key names to know now.

events.title: Mass tanker blackout rattles Gulf ahead of 1.35M-barrel oil transfer amid US-Iran talks: firm
events.description: Maritime tracking signals collapse near the UAE's Fujairah oil hub hours before President Trump announced progress on a bilateral peace deal with Iran, according to an AI maritime firm.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei using ‘bin Laden template’ to survive, learned from Abbottabad: analyst
events.description: Analyst compares Mojtaba Khamenei's disappearance from public view to Osama bin Laden's decade of hiding, calling it a strategic milestone for the U.S.

events.title: 'Written in our DNA': Polish pilots who remember Soviet rule prepare for America's most lethal fighter jet
events.description: Poland expects its first F-35 fighter jets very soon as U.S. and Polish forces train together at a key NATO air base near the alliance's eastern flank.
